Patch Set 6: Looks good to me, but someone else must approve

Patch Set 6:

is it really used in MDD ?

Patch Set 6:

Grep dynlock_handle in mdd directory.

Patch Set 6:

well, I guess it's a bit of out the patch's scope, but:

/* PDO lock is unnecessary for current MDT stack because operations
 * are already protected by ldlm lock */
#define MDD_DISABLE_PDO_LOCK    1

Patch Set 6: No score

Which is a good question. Will this feature be turned on in the future or just be removed? Since we are in bug fixing mode and this could impact a future feature I would consider this patch more a 2.5 thing now.

Patch Set 6:

I think it won't be used as dynlocks are down in ldiskfs (and ZFS does own fine grained locking for ZAP).

Patch Set 6:

In that case it would make more sense to move it to osd-ldiskfs and remove the mdd dynlock handling. This allows ldiskfs then to trace closer to the mainstream ext4 code. We can look into this in 2.5.

Patch Set 6:

hmm, why would we need to move it to osd-ldiskfs ?

Patch Set 6:

just to clarify... dynlocks are not used in MDD or other core Lustre. ldiskfs/iam do have own internal locking. so, i'd think we can just drop this code?

Patch Set 6: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

marking -1 to make it unbold in my list :)

Patch Set 6:

To answer the question why for this patch. For 2.5 I like to make ldiskfs patch less against the tip of the linux kernel tree. Looking at osd_iam.c I still see dynlock_handle being used to protect parts of the directory's htree. Yes this patch needs to updated to reflect the mdd layer not needing the dynlock handling.

Patch Set 6:

> To answer the question why for this patch. For 2.5 I like to make ldiskfs patch less against the tip of the linux kernel tree. Looking at osd_iam.c I still see dynlock_handle being used to protect parts of the directory's htree. Yes this patch needs to updated to reflect the mdd layer not needing the dynlock handling.

then it should be part of osd or ldiskfs, not obdclass/ ?

Patch Set 6:

I agree. It needs to be apart of osd-ldiskfs. New patch will be coming soon.

parents.subject: LU-3986 build: eliminate redundant kernel config files

commit.subject: LU-2757 dynlocks: move into osd-ldiskfs
commit.message:
LU-2757 dynlocks: move into osd-ldiskfs

Commit 43603bd1 (LU-1548 osd: move i_htree_lock to iam container)
eliminated the use of dynlocks inside ldiskfs. Now that it's only
used in the osd-ldiskfs code, we can move it under osd-ldiskfs/,
and eliminate a patch we apply during build. Long term dynlocks
will never merged into the ext4 work so it makes sense to move it
to the osd-ldisk layer. Also the mdd layer was carrying techinical
debt of dynlocks as well so this patch removes it from that layer.
This work helps us move forward to a patchless ldiskfs in the
future.

Signed-off-by: Jeff Mahoney <jeffm@suse.com>
Signed-off-by: James Simmons <uja.ornl@gmail.com>
Change-Id: I3d2ca2317426df3a7f3855d85a834a37091a78bd
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/5282
Reviewed-by: Bob Glossman <bob.glossman@intel.com>
Tested-by: Hudson
Tested-by: Maloo <whamcloud.maloo@gmail.com>
Reviewed-by: Andreas Dilger <andreas.dilger@intel.com>
